question: Is the tip blocked?
Is science only in a lab, or is it sitting beside you?
Before throwing it away, can you solve the mystery like a scientist?
What happens when they share?

Science Starts with Curious Questions",
      "experience": {
        "narration": "Science begins when we feel curious and look carefully. A question is not a weakness. It is the first step of discovery. When we notice something and ask why or how, our mind starts searching for a true answer.",
        "reflection": "Observe: Look at a safe object near you for one minute. Notice its colour, shape, size, texture, or sound.",
        "application": "Read/Write: Write one thing you saw today and one question about it."
      },
      "connection": {
        "mathematical_connection": "",
        "real_world_application": "Local Life: Before monsoon rain, many families observe clouds and wind and ask when it may rain.",
        "conn_title": "Real World Connection"
      },
      "exploration": {
        "title": "Takeaway",
        "take": "Curiosity starts science. Careful noticing helps us ask better questions. Simple questions can lead to real learning.",
        "observe": "",
        "guide": "",
        "body": "Bharatiya Connection: Aryabhata studied the sky carefully and wrote about the movement of heavenly bodies. His work reminds us that big discoveries can begin with a curious question.",
        "watch_out": ""
      },
      "inquiry": {
        "title": "Vocabulary",
        "questions": "Curiosity: A strong wish to know or understand something.",
        "challenge": "Observation: careful noticing using our senses."
      },
      "extensions": [],
      "references": [],
      "image_b64": "data:image/png;base64,"
    },
    {
      "concept_name": "Science Is Everywhere Around Us",
      "experience": {
        "narration": "Science is not only in laboratories. It is in the kitchen, garden, sky, ocean, playground, and school bag. When we ask what things are made of, how water changes, why stars shine, or how plants grow, we are using science thinking.",
        "reflection": "Observe: Watch water in a glass, a plant near a window, or clouds before rain. Write only what you truly see.",
        "application": "Read/Write: Make four boxes: kitchen, sky, garden, school bag. Write one science question for each."
      },
      "connection": {
        "mathematical_connection": "",
        "real_world_application": "Local Life: In an Indian kitchen, boiling milk, steaming rice, and a pressure cooker can raise many science questions.",
        "conn_title": "Real World Connection"
      },
      "exploration": {
        "title": "Takeaway",
        "take": "Science is present in daily life. Every place can give us questions. Nature and objects can both be studied.",
        "observe": "",
        "guide": "",
        "body": "Bharatiya Connection: Charaka carefully observed food, plants, and the human body while writing about health. This shows how science questions can begin from daily life.",
        "watch_out": ""
      },
      "inquiry": {
        "title": "Vocabulary",
        "questions": "Surroundings: Everything around us, such as air, water, plants, animals, objects, sky, and people.",
        "challenge": ""
      },
      "extensions": [],
      "references": [],
      "image_b64": "data:image/png;base64,"
    },
    {
      "concept_name": "Finding Answers Step by Step",
      "experience": {
        "narration": "In science, we do not jump to the answer. We observe, ask a question, guess a possible answer, test the guess, and analyse what happened. This step-by-step way helps us solve small and big problems.",
        "reflection": "Do: Choose one safe classroom problem, such as a pencil point breaking. Think of two possible reasons.",
        "application": "Read/Write: Write the five steps for the pen problem: observe, question, guess, test, analyse."
      },
      "connection": {
        "mathematical_connection": "",
        "real_world_application": "Think Carefully: The refill is empty is a guess. Opening and checking the refill is a test.",
        "conn_title": "Real World Connection"
      },
      "exploration": {
        "title": "Takeaway",
        "take": "Science follows steps. Testing helps us check a guess. Analysing results helps us learn honestly.",
        "observe": "",
        "guide": "",
        "body": "Bharatiya Connection: Jagadish Chandra Bose used careful experiments to test his ideas. He showed that science grows stronger when guesses are checked properly.",
        "watch_out": ""
      },
      "inquiry": {
        "title": "Vocabulary",
        "questions": "Scientific method: A step-by-step way to find answers to questions.",
        "challenge": "Experiment: a test or activity done to check an idea."
      },
      "extensions": [],
      "references": [],
      "image_b64": "data:image/png;base64,"
    },
    {
      "concept_name": "We Can All Work Like Scientists",
      "experience": {
        "narration": "A scientist uses careful questions, observations, tests, and thinking to find answers. But science is not only for famous people. A cook, repair person, student, farmer, or electrician can work like a scientist when they solve problems step by step.",
        "reflection": "Observe: With a partner, look at the same safe object for one minute. Compare what each of you noticed.",
        "application": "Read/Write: Write one question. Ask a friend to add one more observation to your question."
      },
      "connection": {
        "mathematical_connection": "",
        "real_world_application": "Local Life: A bicycle repair person checks where air leaks before fixing a flat tyre.",
        "conn_title": "Real World Connection"
      },
      "exploration": {
        "title": "Takeaway",
        "take": "Anyone can think like a scientist. Sharing observations can improve answers. Honesty and patience are important in science.",
        "observe": "",
        "guide": "",
        "body": "Bharatiya Connection: ISRO missions such as Chandrayaan need many people to observe, test, improve, and work as a team. Science becomes stronger when people share ideas carefully.",
        "watch_out": ""
      },
      "inquiry": {
        "title": "Vocabulary",
        "questions": "Scientist: A person who uses careful steps to discover or solve something.",
        "challenge": ""
      },
      "extensions": [],
      "references": [],
      "image_b64": "data:image/png;base64,"
    }
  ],
  "light_assessment_cards": [
    {
      "question": "MCQ: A child sees ants carrying food and asks, 'Where are they taking it?
What is the child showing?
Kitchen: Why does steam rise?
Sky: Why do stars shine?
Garden: How does a seed grow?
School bag: What is a ruler made of?

Start with what you can see before you guess.",
        "guide": "Compare your pen with a working pen and write what is different.",
        "body": "Which clue is strongest: ink level, dry tip, broken refill, or paper problem?
Why do you think so?
Which clue is strongest?
